reason: Birth advisory message-string rows for the >1-nested-candidate case in handleIntentBirthStateBuild. Reaching this advisory requires driving a full intent birth, which needs a completely seeded workspace (memory/rules) — canonical in-process birth is impractical (no-canonical-direct-execution), and error()/die paths in birth are process.exit. The IDENTICAL not-auto-selected condition on the sibling detect surface (handleDetect) IS covered in-process by tests/integration/t249-canonical-nested-advisory; this is the same additive stdout advisory row. Message-string rows are the named second-resort allowlist case in spawn-blindspot-two-step (ii).
expiry: remove when a canonical in-process intent-birth harness (seeded workspace) drives the multiple-nested advisory

reason: Defensive `catch {}` in findActiveStandingGrant that returns null so a verification defect can never wedge a gate (design contract E-SDG: throws collapse to per-gate approval, never fail-open at the check). No natural corpus input throws inside the scan (listIntentDirs / readAllAuditShards / StandingGrant.parse / verifyDelegatedProvenance all swallow their own IO errors), so the catch arm is unreachable by a black-box test; the happy scan and every classification branch are covered in-process by t-standing-grant.
expiry: remove if the scan grows a throw path a test can inject
